entry_transition.notes: Dissolve from frantic temple escape (M14 kinetic energy) to sudden stillness at empty finish line. Contrast between chase urgency and eerie quiet amplifies emotional shift from triumph to isolation.

exit_handoff.leaves_on: Player frozen in indecision at empty finish line, distant fireworks bursting in sky (opposite direction). Body language shifts from confusion (pose_01 look_around) to resolve — weight transferring toward the direction of celebration sounds. The white finish line remains visible but abandoned, no longer the goal.
exit_handoff.next_scene_should_open_with: Player mid-stride running TOWARD the distant celebration (away from empty finish line). Chosen tool from M15 (flag/megaphone/firework) visible in hand or being used. Transition from solitude to community — sounds of crowd/music growing louder, lighting warmer. If final mission: arrival at true gathering, realization that connection mattered more than winning.

mood_arc: Sharp tonal pivot from M14's adrenaline-fueled escape to M15's existential pause. Player arrives victorious but alone — the empty finish line and distant celebration create cognitive dissonance that forces choice between hollow achievement and authentic belonging.
visual_callbacks: Treasure from M14 still clutched in hand (continuity of 'winning' the game)
Tropical jungle palette maintained but lighting shifts to golden-hour warmth (time passage from temple interior to exterior finale)
Ground-POV camera angle consistent with M14's player-perspective immersion
rationale: M14 ends mid-escape with treasure secured; M15 opens post-arrival at intended goal. The 600ms cross-dissolve bridges the implied journey while emphasizing the emotional whiplash — from 'I won!' to 'Did I win the wrong thing?' The exit handoff prepares for either final mission resolution (joining celebration) or reflective denouement, leaving player poised between isolation and community.
